content: Icons are graphical elements representing various functions: Energy Icon, Mental Icon, Physical Icon, Wild Icon (resource icons); Acceleration Icon; Amplify Icon; Crisis Icon; Hazard Icon; Boost Icon; Star Icon; Consequential Damage Icon; Cost Arrow; Per Player Icon; Unique Icon.

Identity is a player card type representing which character a player is playing.

  • A player's identity card is double-sided (hero side, alter-ego side); the faceup side indicates current form.
  • Each player begins the game in alter-ego form.
  • If a card refers to a hero or alter-ego by title, it refers only to that side, not the other. Identity cards cannot be discarded from play.
  • The faceup side is in play; the facedown side is out of play.

• Cards in the 'identity-specific' classification (also 'hero-specific') belong to an identity's set of accompanying cards.
  • A player's deck must include each identity-specific card associated with their chosen identity in the exact quantity. Identity-specific cards (with obligations and nemesis set cards) may only be used alongside an identity sharing a set icon. Designated by the identity icon in the bottom right corner; identity cards are identity-specific cards.

An ability that ignores some ability, icon, or cost treats it as not being in effect or present while resolving. When a card is played 'ignoring its resource cost,' no resources are paid; for card effects, it's considered played with zero resources paid.

term: In Play and Out of Play
content:
• A card is either in play or out of play depending on its state. In play means its text is active and can affect the game: faceup identity cards, faceup ally/support/upgrade cards in play, top card of villain deck/main scheme deck, faceup attachment/environment/minion/obligation/side scheme cards.
  • A card enters play when moving from out-of-play to a play area.
  • Card abilities only interact with/target in-play cards unless specified otherwise.
  • Card abilities on all types except events and treacheries can only be initiated while in play unless specified. Out of play means text is inactive: events/treacheries implicitly resolve from out-of-play; hand/deck/discard piles; encounter deck/discard pile, unrevealed villain/main scheme cards, facedown dealt encounter cards; facedown cards attached to in-play cards; removed from game or set aside cards.

If players perform a sequence 'in player order,' the first player performs their part first, followed by others in clockwise order. If it doesn't conclude after one full pass, it continues clockwise until complete. 'Next player' refers to the next clockwise player.

When a card with the incite X keyword is revealed, place X threat on the main scheme.

  • Equivalent to a triggered ability:
    When Revealed: Place X threat on the main scheme.

• Some card abilities deal 'indirect damage.' Dealt to a player, it can be divided among characters they control; dealt to a group of players, divided among friendly characters in play.
  • All indirect damage from a single source is first assigned then resolved simultaneously; while assigning, a character cannot be assigned more than would defeat it (assessed without other ability interactions).
  • A tough status card character can be assigned indirect damage up to remaining hit points, prevented by the tough card. Characters that cannot take damage cannot be assigned indirect damage; if it cannot be assigned to any controlled character, it's ignored.
  • If an enemy attack deals indirect damage, it's dealt during step four of the activation (after defense opportunity).

When a player wishes to play a card or initiate a triggered ability, they declare intent, then:

  1. If playing a card, place it faceup on the table (not in play).
  2. Check play restrictions: valid target(s) exist and form requirement met.
  3. Determine cost(s) and ability to pay, with modifiers. If both conditions met:.
  4. Apply cost modifiers.
  5. Pay the cost(s); if unable, abort without paying.
  6. The card/ability attempts to initiate.
  7. The card is played or the ability resolves; the card enters play or (event) resolves and is discarded. If any step would make an interrupt's triggering condition true, that interrupt may initiate just before it becomes true; similarly for responses immediately after. If the ability is on a card in play, the sequence completes even if the card leaves play during the sequence, unless that prevents a required cost from being paid.

• An interrupt ability is a triggered ability indicated by the bold 'Interrupt' timing trigger, resolved anytime the specified triggering condition occurs, interrupting resolution of that condition and resolving immediately before it resolves.
  • Players can only trigger interrupt abilities on cards they control or encounter cards (not obligations in other players' play areas).
  • Multiple interrupts may trigger from the same condition but each interrupt only once per occurrence; multiple copies of a card can each trigger. Interrupts using 'would' resolve before the triggering condition initiates, when imminent.
  • If an interrupt changes or cancels an imminent triggering condition (via replacement effect), further interrupts to the original trigger cannot be triggered.
  • Once all players decline further interrupts to a triggering condition, further interrupts to that instance cannot be used.
